description: Bootstrap the full project foundation so that all subsequent development phases can begin from a clean, reproducible baseline. This epic covers the Nx monorepo and Next.js frontend skeleton, Spring Boot microservice module initialization, local development infrastructure, baseline architecture documentation, and GCP Terraform scaffolding. Exit criteria: the application boots locally, all five architecture documents are in place, and core package and naming conventions (xyz.catuns.onboarding) are fixed.
functionality: bootstrap the full monorepo, service skeletons, local development stack, architecture documentation, and Terraform scaffolding
benefit: every subsequent phase starts from a clean, reproducible baseline with no naming, structure, or environment ambiguity

stories.summary: Initialize Nx monorepo with Next.js frontend skeleton
stories.description: Set up the <frontend/> Nx monorepo using pnpm. Scaffold the Next.js application under <frontend/apps/web/> with TypeScript and create page stubs for /register, /onboarding, /dashboard, and /support. Add the <frontend/features/auth/> library with Auth.js (NextAuth) wired for GitHub OAuth and Credentials providers. Configure the Zod-validated env schema in <frontend/apps/web/lib/config/env.ts> with BACKEND_API_URL as a required variable. The dev server must start cleanly.

acceptanceCriteria.given: the frontend/ directory is set up with pnpm and Nx
acceptanceCriteria.when: pnpm nx run web:serve is executed
acceptanceCriteria.then: the Next.js dev server starts without errors and the /register page is reachable

acceptanceCriteria.given: the features/auth library is added to the Nx workspace
acceptanceCriteria.when: pnpm nx run web:build is executed
acceptanceCriteria.then: the build completes successfully with no TypeScript errors

acceptanceCriteria.given: BACKEND_API_URL is missing from the environment
acceptanceCriteria.then: a Zod validation error is thrown identifying the missing variable

tasks.description: Initialise the Nx monorepo under frontend/ using pnpm as the package manager and configure nx.json with the correct project layout.
Add the web app under frontend/apps/web/ with TypeScript, configure tsconfig, and create page stubs for /register, /onboarding, /dashboard, and /support.

tasks.summary: Add auth feature library with GitHub OAuth
tasks.description: Create frontend/features/auth/ as an Nx library, install Auth.js (NextAuth), and wire GitHub OAuth and Credentials providers with JWT callbacks capturing githubId, githubLogin, and githubAccessToken.
Add frontend/apps/web/lib/config/env.ts with a Zod schema that validates all required environment variables including BACKEND_API_URL at startup.
Create frontend/apps/web/.env.example listing all required environment variables with placeholder values and inline comments.

stories.description: Create the three Spring Boot Maven modules under <backend/>: user-service, onboarding-service, and provisioning-service. Each module must use the package root xyz.catuns.onboarding, include the correct Spring Boot starters (Web, Data JPA, Kafka, Actuator, Validation, PostgreSQL driver), define a minimal application.yml stub, and compile and start without errors. The Maven multi-module parent POM must define shared dependency management.

acceptanceCriteria.given: the backend Maven multi-module project is set up
acceptanceCriteria.when: ./mvnw clean package is run from any service directory
acceptanceCriteria.then: all three services compile with no errors

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Service starts with actuator health endpoint
acceptanceCriteria.given: a local Postgres instance is available
acceptanceCriteria.then: the service starts and GET /actuator/health returns {"status": "UP"}

acceptanceCriteria.given: any Spring Boot service source file

tasks.summary: Create Maven parent POM with dependency management
tasks.description: Add backend/pom.xml as the multi-module parent defining Spring Boot version, shared dependency management, and module declarations for user-service, onboarding-service, and provisioning-service.

Create backend/user-service/ with Maven wrapper, src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/user/ package structure, UserServiceApplication main class, and application.yml stub with datasource and server port placeholders.
Create backend/onboarding-service/ with Maven wrapper, src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/onboarding/ package structure, OnboardingServiceApplication main class, and application.yml stub.
Create backend/provisioning-service/ with Maven wrapper, src/main/java/xyz/catuns/onboarding/provisioning/ package structure, ProvisioningServiceApplication main class, and application.yml stub.

tasks.summary: Add Spring Boot starters to each service
tasks.description: Add spring-boot-starter-web, spring-boot-starter-data-jpa, spring-boot-starter-actuator, spring-boot-starter-validation, spring-kafka, and postgresql driver dependencies to each service POM.

stories.summary: Configure local development infrastructure with Docker Compose
stories.description: Create a Docker Compose file under <infra/docker/> that starts a PostgreSQL 15 container with three databases auto-initialised (user_service, onboarding_service, provisioning_service), a Kafka broker in KRaft mode on port 9092, and a Confluent Schema Registry on port 9090. Provide <infra/docker/.env.example> listing all required environment variables. All Spring Boot services must be able to connect using the values in the example env file.

acceptanceCriteria.given: Docker is running and infra/docker/.env is populated from .env.example
acceptanceCriteria.when: docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d is executed
acceptanceCriteria.then: all containers reach a healthy or running state with no startup errors

acceptanceCriteria.when: a connection is attempted to user_service, onboarding_service, and provisioning_service databases
acceptanceCriteria.then: all three databases accept connections using the configured credentials

acceptanceCriteria.given: the Kafka container is running in KRaft mode
acceptanceCriteria.when: a producer client connects to localhost:9092
acceptanceCriteria.then: the connection is accepted and topic metadata can be retrieved

acceptanceCriteria.given: the Schema Registry container is running
acceptanceCriteria.then: the response is HTTP 200 with a JSON array

tasks.summary: Write docker-compose.yml with Postgres, Kafka, Schema Registry
tasks.description: Create infra/docker/docker-compose.yml defining services for postgres (image: postgres:15), kafka (KRaft mode), and schema-registry (Confluent), with correct port mappings and health checks.

tasks.summary: Add Postgres init scripts for three databases
tasks.description: Create infra/docker/initdb/ SQL scripts that CREATE DATABASE user_service, onboarding_service, and provisioning_service during Postgres container first-start.
Set KAFKA_PROCESS_ROLES, KAFKA_NODE_ID, KAFKA_CONTROLLER_QUORUM_VOTERS, and related KRaft environment variables so Kafka starts without ZooKeeper on port 9092.
Add infra/docker/.env.example with placeholder values for POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS, and any other variables consumed by the Compose file.

stories.description: Create and finalize the five authoritative documentation files under <docs/>. Each file must accurately reflect the current architecture decisions, naming conventions, and contracts that all subsequent phases will implement against. The documents are: design-spec.md (architecture decisions and hard constraints), request-flow.md (end-to-end flow descriptions), rest-api-reference.md (all REST endpoint shapes and status codes), schemas/normalized-database-schema.md (canonical DB schema), and schemas/kafka-event-schema.md (Avro schemas, topic names, and compatibility rules).

acceptanceCriteria.when: each required file path is checked
acceptanceCriteria.then: design-spec.md, request-flow.md, rest-api-reference.md, schemas/normalized-database-schema.md, and schemas/kafka-event-schema.md all exist and are non-empty
the entry point is documented as POST /api/v1/registrations matching the CLAUDE.md hard constraint

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Kafka schema file defines topic names
acceptanceCriteria.then: edu.user.registered.v1, edu.identity.correlation.v1, edu.provisioning.github.v1, edu.provisioning.atlassian.v1, and edu.onboarding.lifecycle.v1 are all documented

tasks.description: Document architecture decisions, service boundaries, hard constraints (async provisioning, outbox pattern, auth is frontend-only), and acceptance criteria for each phase.
Document the end-to-end happy path and failure flows including GitHub OAuth, registration, identity correlation, provisioning, and frontend polling.
Document all REST endpoints with request/response shapes, HTTP status codes, and error envelope format. Cover all endpoints owned by user-service and onboarding-service.
Document the canonical DB schema for all tables across user_service, onboarding_service, and provisioning_service databases including column types, constraints, and foreign keys.
Document all Avro schemas under namespace xyz.catuns.onboarding.events, topic names, subject naming conventions, and backward-compatibility rules.

stories.description: Create Terraform module scaffolding under <infra/terraform/> for the GCP deployment target. The scaffolding must include module stubs for Cloud Run service definitions (user-service, onboarding-service, provisioning-service, and the web app), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL, Kafka infrastructure (Confluent Cloud or Pub/Sub bridge), Secret Manager secrets for provider credentials (GitHub token, Atlassian API key), IAM service accounts with least-privilege bindings, and environment-specific variable files for dev and prod. All modules must pass terraform validate. No secrets or credentials may be hardcoded.

acceptanceCriteria.given: infra/terraform/ is populated with module scaffolding
acceptanceCriteria.when: terraform init is run from the root Terraform directory
acceptanceCriteria.then: all providers and modules initialise with no errors
the configuration is valid with no errors or warnings

acceptanceCriteria.given: the Terraform source files are inspected
acceptanceCriteria.when: any file is checked for credential values
acceptanceCriteria.then: all secrets reference Secret Manager data sources or input variables with sensitive = true; no literal credentials appear
separate .tfvars files exist for dev and prod environments

tasks.summary: Create Terraform root module and backend config
tasks.description: Add infra/terraform/main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, and versions.tf with required GCP provider version, backend config pointing to GCS for remote state, and project/region variables.

tasks.summary: Add Cloud Run module stubs for each service
tasks.description: Create Terraform modules for each Cloud Run service (user-service, onboarding-service, provisioning-service, web) with image, env vars (sourced from Secret Manager), scaling config, and service account binding placeholders.
Create a Cloud SQL module stub for PostgreSQL 15, private IP networking, automated backups, and database/user provisioning for user_service, onboarding_service, and provisioning_service.

tasks.summary: Add Secret Manager module for provider credentials
tasks.description: Define Secret Manager secret resources for GITHUB_TOKEN, ATLASSIAN_API_KEY, ATLASSIAN_ACCOUNT_EMAIL, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, and any other runtime secrets. Reference these in Cloud Run env var definitions.

tasks.summary: Add IAM service accounts with least-privilege bindings
tasks.description: Create service accounts for each Cloud Run service and bind only the IAM roles required: Secret Manager Secret Accessor, Cloud SQL Client, and Pub/Sub roles as appropriate per service.

tasks.summary: Create dev and prod tfvars files
tasks.description: Add infra/terraform/environments/dev.tfvars and infra/terraform/environments/prod.tfvars with environment-specific values for project ID, region, instance sizing, and replica counts.
Ensure infra/terraform/.gitignore excludes .tfstate, .tfstate.backup, .tfvars containing secrets, and the .terraform/ directory. Commit the .terraform.lock.hcl provider lock file.
